articles.title: THE INTERFACE IS NO LONGER THE PRODUCT
articles.summary: Agent-native software shifts the product from the interface to the structured source of truth underneath it. The winners will own the artifact layer that humans and agents can inspect, update, validate, and render into docs, decks, dashboards, or workflows.

articles.title: WHY REAL-TIME INFERENCE IS THE NEW PRODUCT BATTLEGROUND
articles.summary: Real-time inference is now a product requirement, not just an infrastructure choice. PMs should set clear latency goals and design AI features that feel instant, seamless, and reliable.

articles.title: THE BEST PROTOTYPES GET THROWN AWAY
articles.summary: The best prototypes are built to answer a decision, then thrown away. Teams should avoid over-polishing or piling up prototypes, and instead match fidelity to the question they need to answer.

articles.title: YOU'LL LOSE YOUR JOB IN 2027
articles.summary: Your job may look very different by 2027, so start preparing now. Build career optionality, sharpen your AI skills, and decide what kind of work you actually want to do next.

articles.title: STATE OF AI-DRIVEN SOFTWARE RELEASES 2026
articles.summary: AI is accelerating software delivery, but most teams are still adapting their release processes. Harness and LeadDev surveyed 500 engineering leaders on AI-generated code, experimentation, governance, and release guardrails.

articles.title: WHAT IS ICP IN MARKETING? A PM'S GUIDE TO GROWTH
WHY UX SHOULD OWN RETENTION
articles.summary: UX should own retention because churn often starts with a broken customer experience. By improving onboarding, support, renewals, and upgrades, UX teams can protect revenue and prove their strategic value.

articles.title: WHY DEFINING TEAMS IS SO HARD
articles.summary: Teams are hard to define because the org chart rarely matches how work actually gets done. The real challenge is naming the messy dependencies, incentives, architecture, and history that everyone sees but few teams feel safe enough to discuss.

articles.title: WHICH LIES ARE YOU TELLING YOURSELF?
articles.summary: PMs are less powerless than they think. By challenging stale assumptions, gathering evidence, finding ways to reach customers, and framing ideas around business priorities, they can influence roadmaps, leaders, and cross-functional decisions.

articles.title: YOUR DEFECT BACKLOG IS A RETENTION REPORT
articles.summary: Defect backlogs are retention risks, not just engineering chores.

articles.title: FROM PERFORMANCE REVIEWS TO PINK SLIPS, MANAGING AI AGENTS LOOKS A LOT LIKE MANAGING PEOPLE
articles.summary: IBM is treating AI agents like digital employees that need lifecycle management, credentials, oversight, and retirement plans.
